---
title: ""
---

<div class="hero">

<img src="photo_profile.jpeg" class="profile-pic">

<div>

# Faadil Mustun

<p class="subtitle">
PhD Candidate · Univ. Paris Cité / ENS Paris
</p>

<div class="contact-links">
<a href="mailto:faadilmustun@hotmail.fr"><i class="bi bi-envelope-fill"></i> Email</a>
<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=yCws0CEAAAAJ&hl=en"><i class="bi bi-mortarboard-fill"></i> Scholar</a>
<a href="https://github.com/fmustun"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
</div>

</div>

</div>


## About

I am a PhD candidate at the Institut de Biologie de l'École Normale Supérieure (IBENS) and Université Paris Cité. 
I hold a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics from Université Pierre et Marie Curie and a Master's degree in Applied Mathematics (Statistics) from Sorbonne Université.

My research lies at the intersection of bioacoustics, animal communication, and machine learning. Using large-scale longitudinal acoustic datasets and self-supervised learning approaches, I investigate how dolphin vocalizations encode information about identity, social relationships, and interaction dynamics. 
More broadly, I am interested in developing computational tools to uncover the principles underlying animal communication systems and their evolution.
More recently, I have been contributing to the development of multimodal datasets combining acoustic recordings with behavioral annotations extracted from video data.

## News

- **24/03/2026** — Presented my work to the Neurosection Department Seminar, Institut de Biologie, ENS Paris.
- **03/02/2026** — Launched the citizen science project [Dolphin Spotting](https://www.zooniverse.org/projects/sumbredolphin/dolphin-spotting) on Zooniverse to collect behavioral annotations from dolphin video recordings.
- **12/12/2025** — Our paper [Dolph2Vec](https://openreview.net/forum?id=QGAFX5kcR5) got accepted at the AI for Non-Human Animal Communication workshop @ NeurIPS 2025.
- **14/10/2024** — Presented my research on dolphins with the general public at the Fête de la Science in Paris!
- **21/03/2023** — Gave a guest lecture on dolphin acoustic communication @ Ben Gurion Univ., Eilat Campus.


## Publications

::: {.columns .pub-entry}

::: {.column width="30%"}

![](images/openwhistle.png){width=100%}

:::

::: {.column width="70%"}

### OpenWhistle

**F. Mustun**, C. Semenzin, R. Dessì, P. Robin Guerrero, P. Orhan, A. Emanuelli, E. Rossi, Y. Lakretz, G. de Polavieja, G. Sumbre.

*OpenWhistle: A Large-Scale Longitudinal Dataset and Benchmark of Bottlenose Dolphin Vocalizations.* Under review.

OpenWhistle introduces a large-scale longitudinal dataset of approximately 180,000 bottlenose dolphin whistles recorded over five years. The dataset includes expert annotations and standardized benchmarks for whistle detection and classification, providing a resource for the development and evaluation of machine learning methods in dolphin bioacoustics.


:::

:::

---

::: {.columns .pub-entry}

::: {.column width="30%"}

![](images/dolph2vec.png){width=100%}

:::

::: {.column width="70%"}

### Dolph2Vec

C. Semenzin, **F. Mustun**, R. Dessì, A. Emanuelli, P. Orhan, Y. Lakretz, G. de Polavieja, G. Sumbre.

*Dolph2Vec: Self-Supervised Representations of Dolphin Vocalizations.* AI for Non-Human Animal Communication Workshop @ NeurIPS 2025.

Dolph2Vec is a self-supervised learning framework trained directly on dolphin vocalizations. The learned representations outperform general-purpose bioacoustic embeddings on dolphin-specific tasks and capture biologically meaningful acoustic structure.

[Paper](https://openreview.net/forum?id=QGAFX5kcR5) · [Code](https://github.com/chiarasemenzin/Dolph2Vec)

:::

:::

---

::: {.columns .pub-entry}

::: {.column width="30%"}

![](images/variability.png){width=100%}

:::

::: {.column width="70%"}

### Signature Whistle Variability

**F. Mustun* **, C. Semenzin*, D. Rance, E. Marachlian, Z. Guillerm, A. Mancini, I. Bouaziz, E. Fleck, N. Shashar, G. de Polavieja, G. Sumbre.

*Whistle Variability and Social Acoustic Interactions in Bottlenose Dolphins.* Under review.

This work shows that dolphin signature whistles exhibit structured within-individual variability rather than stereotyped production. By modeling transitions between whistle variants, we reveal non-random acoustic interaction patterns that form a modular social communication network.

[Preprint](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2024.10.15.618471v1) · [Code](https://github.com/zebrain-lab/Dolphins)

:::

:::

---

## Publications

1. **F. Mustun**, C. Semenzin, R. Dessì, P. Robin Guerrero, P. Orhan, A. Emanuelli, E. Rossi, Y. Lakretz, G. de Polavieja, G. Sumbre. 
   *OpenWhistle: A Large-Scale Longitudinal Dataset and Benchmark of Bottlenose Dolphin Vocalizations.* Under review.
2. Semenzin, C., **Mustun, F.**, Dessì, R., Emanuelli, A., Orhan, P., Lakretz, Y., de Polavieja, G., Sumbre, G. (2025). 
   *Dolph2Vec: Self-Supervised Representations of Dolphin Vocalizations.* [Preprint](https://openreview.net/forum?id=QGAFX5kcR5)
3. **Mustun, F.* **, Semenzin, C.\*, Rance, D., Marachlian, E., Guillerm, Z., Mancini, A., Bouaziz, I., Fleck, E., Shashar, N., de Polavieja, G., Sumbre, G. (2024).
   *Whistle variability and social acoustic interactions in bottlenose dolphins.* Under review. [Preprint](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2024.10.15.618471v1)


## Selected Talks & Posters

- 2025 — AI for Non-Human Animal Communication workshop @ NeurIPS 2025, San Diego
- 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026 — Neurosection Department Seminar, Institut de Biologie, ENS Paris
- 2023 — Protolang, Rome
- 2023, 2024 — NeuroFrance, Lyon 
- 2022 — International Congress for Neuroethology, Lisbon

## Education

- **PhD Student** — Université Paris Cité / École Normale Supériure - Paris, 2022-Present
- **Master’s Degree in Applied Mathematics (Specialization: Statistics)** — Sorbonne Universités - Paris, 2019
- **Bachelor’s Degree in Mathematics** — Université Pierre et Marie Curie - Paris, 2016

## Research Experience

**PhD Candidate**, Institut de Biologie de l'École Normale Supérieure (IBENS), Université Paris Cité, Paris, France *(2022–Present)*  
Research on dolphin communication, bioacoustics, and machine learning.

**Statistical Engineer**, INSERM / IBENS, Paris, France *(2021–2022)*  
Developed computational methods for dolphin vocalization detection, classification, and sequence analysis.

**Data Science Intern**, Audentiel Conseil R&D, Boulogne-Billancourt, France *(2019)*  
Worked on hyperparameter optimization and machine learning model selection.
